description:
'''Incelcore''' is an [[Internet rock|Internet]] [[microgenre]] of [[rock music]] and [[online subculture]] that emerged in 2019 with the release of the compilation album ''MSG Vol. 1''. The style was originally pioneered by musician and Internet personality [[Negative XP]] (formerly known as School Shooter), drawing influence from [[Punk rock|punk]], [[Electronic music|electronic]] and [[alternative rock]].      

According to the [[Canadian Anti-Hate Network]], not all artists and fans identify with the [[incel movement]]; however, they evoke the same themes of [[Self-hatred|self-hate]], [[hopelessness]] and [[misogyny]]. In 2021, an incelcore concert known as "[[Virginfest]]", self-described as an "[[incel]] music festival", was hosted by Athena Raven Rapp in [[Atlanta, Georgia]], with Negative XP billed as a headliner. Music publication ''[[Pitchfork (website)|Pitchfork]]'' described incelcore as "dour or woe-is-me or stupidly [[Edgelord|edgy]]," with lyrics inspired by [[4chan]]'s speaking culture.     

Notable artists include Fried by Fluoride, Negative XP, Gezebelle Gaburgably and UhOhSlater.
